commentaries.author: Clement Of Alexandria
commentaries.content: Dearly beloved, I beseech you as aliens and exiles, abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul; Qu circa admirabilis quoque Petrus: "Charis Simi, inquit, obsecro vos tanquam advernas et peregrinos, abstinete vos a carnal bus desideriis, quae militant ad versus animam, conversationem vest ram inter gentes habentes bonam: quoniam sic est voluntas Dei, ut bene facientes obmutescere faciatis imprudentium hominum ignorant am; quasi liberi, et non quasi velamen habentes malitiae libertatem, sed ut servi Dei.

commentaries.author: Cyprian of Carthage
commentaries.content: And similarly Peter exhorts: "As strangers "says he, "and pilgrims, abstain from fleshly lusts, which war against the soul, having your conversation honest among the Gentiles; that whereas they speak against you as evil-doers, they may by your good works, which they shall behold, glorify the Lord."

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: I beseech you to refrain from all unlawful and disorderly passions, that the Gentiles not yet converted may have nothing to blame in your lives and conversation, but may be edified and induced to praise God. (Witham)

commentaries.author: Polycarp of Smyrna
commentaries.content: The lusts that are in the world, since "every lust warreth against the spirit; "

commentaries.author: The Apostolic Constitutions
commentaries.content: Avoid not the persons, but the sentiments, of the wicked. "Abstain from fleshly and worldly lusts."

commentaries.author: The Didache
commentaries.content: 4. Abstain thou from fleshly and worldly lusts.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
